title: 荣誉附件正文｜LOA full（第9部分）
content:

However, such one pass incremental sentiment analysis still remains an open and challenging problem; 2) despite the success of prior works to achieve high accuracy in sentiment analysis, we observe that they are mostly based on supervised learning paradigm. A fully unsupervised algorithms are highly desired but are less studied for sentiment analysis; 3) we need to handle large volume of data streams during sentiment analysis. For example, there are around 10,000 tweets per second according to twitter usage statistics [15]. Although general-purpose stream processing systems such as Apache Flink [4] that can provide fast stream computations do exist, it still remains an open question of how to leverage them to achieve high performance sentiment analysis. For example, how to efficiently manage and update the model over a parallel and distributed environment. To address the aforementioned challenges, we design a fully end- to-end online sentiment learning framework. Specifically, our framework can incrementally learn vector representation of each new word from data streams efficiently to address the first challenge. Based on computing similarities between a list of reference words and the learned vector representation of each word in a tweet, we determine the polarity of each tweet without manual labelling efforts to address the second challenge. To address the third challenge of handling high velocity of input streams, we implement the proposed algorithm based on Apache Flink and rely on Apache Redis to manage the learned models. Necessary system-oriented optimization techniques (e. g. , model merging) are applied to improve the training and prediction performance. • Competitors/people working in similar areas that the applicant is aware of.
